thread.title: Minnesota elects the nation’s first Kenyan-born state legislator
thread.title_full: Minnesota elects the nation’s first Kenyan-born state legislator

author: The Bharat Express News
title: Minnesota elects the nation’s first Kenyan-born state legislator
text:
Minnesota elects its first Kenyan-born representative to the State House
Minnesota elects its first Kenyan-born representative to the State House
02:21
BROOKLYN PARK, Minn. – Huldah Momanyi-Hiltsley is the first Kenyan-born state legislator in the country. The Democrat will represent Osseo and parts of Brooklyn Park.
“IT IS STUNNING AND HUMBLE” – This Newly Elected State Representative Makes NATIONAL History – Huldah…
Posted by WCCO & CBS News Minnesota on Friday, November 22, 2024
Momanyi-Hiltsley says her dream of becoming a U.S. citizen almost didn’t come true.
“We were literally 48 hours away from deportation from this country,” Momanyi-Hiltsley said.
She moved to Minnesota at the age of nine — her family makes the more than 8,000-mile journey from Nyamira County in Kenya. But after 11 years working through the U.S. immigration system, it was a last-minute intervention by then-Senator Paul Wellstone that made the difference.
“Our case was overturned then and I could not have imagined that I would be interviewing here more than 22 years later as the newly elected representative for this district,” Momanyi-Hiltsley said.
The district that includes Brooklyn Park and Osseo in the Minnesota House has a special place for Momanyi-Hiltsley. It’s an area she considers another home after attending Cooper High School in nearby New Hope.
But news of her election victory leads to high-profile invitations to her home country.
“They invited me to come with my family for a homecoming party,” she said. “There is a possibility of even meeting the President of Kenya and the First Lady for a state visit.”
The victory here in Minnesota also creates a sense of pride or ‘kiburi’ in Swahili among Kenyans.
“We believe in her,” said Duke Maigna Echate, who grew up with her in Kenya. “We are going to support her both mentally and socially.”
A support system that this woman and mother of two says led her to her own American dream.
“I sit here as a testament to what this country stands for and the freedoms,” Momanyi-Hiltsley added.
More from CBS News
